summary: Completed a Thunder item decomp slice to 100% for several functions and data in src/melee/it/items/itthunder. The PR added Thunder-specific item variable and special-attribute structs, wired the Thunder item variable union member, replaced multiple placeholder declarations with typed prototypes, defined the Thunder item state table, and implemented motion/physics helper routines for Thunder behavior, hitlag effect pause/resume, accessory effect spawning, and upward movement/blast-zone termination logic. The match report credited 5 new matches, including itThunder_UnkMotion2_Anim, itThunder_UnkMotion0_Phys, itThunder_UnkMotion1_Anim, it_802CCC68, and .data, plus partial improvements for it_802CCBF8 and itThunder_UnkMotion2_Phys.
what_changed: Added Thunder-specific structs in itCommonItems.h: itThunder_ItemVars with padded layout corresponding to item xDD4/xDD8 storage, and itThunderPokemonAttributes with four fields x0, x4, x8, and xC.
Added itThunder_ItemVars thunder to the Item.xDD4_itemVar union in src/melee/it/types.h so Thunder code can access item->xDD4_itemVar.thunder.x60/x64/x68.
Defined ItemStateTable it_803F7C40 in itthunder.c with three Thunder motion states mapped to UnkMotion0, UnkMotion1, and UnkMotion2 anim/phys/coll callbacks.
Changed Thunder attribute usage in it_2725_Logic7_Spawned from itThunderAttributes to itThunderPokemonAttributes.
Implemented itThunder_UnkMotion1_Anim using grandchild JObj traversal, it_8027A160, it_80272C6C, and fallback transition to it_802CCC68.
Implemented it_802CCBF8 as an accessory callback that counts down Thunder item var x60 while x64 is set, then clears x64 and spawns effect 0x460 via efSync_Spawn on dynamic bone table bone 2.

Implemented it_802CCC68 to switch to motion state 2, set hitlag pause/resume callbacks to efLib_PauseAll and efLib_ResumeAll, initialize Thunder velocity/var x68 from it_804DD3A8, and clear xDB0_itcmd_var1.

Implemented itThunder_UnkMotion2_Anim with shared JObj child traversal helper, animation update through it_8027A160, fallback reinitialization when it_80272C6C fails, and a true return when item->pos.y exceeds Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset().

Implemented itThunder_UnkMotion2_Phys partially/matching-improved: calls it_8027A344, initializes x68 from special attributes when xDAC_itcmd_var0 is set, increments x68 by attrs->x8 while xDB0_itcmd_var1 is active, then assigns item->x40_vel.y from x68.

Implemented itThunder_UnkMotion0_Phys and helpers to respond to it_8027A09C, call it_80273454, set countdown/effect flags from attrs->xC, switch to motion state 1, set hitlag pause/resume callbacks, install it_802CCBF8 as on_accessory, and set xDD1_flag.b1.
Updated itthunder.h prototypes for it_802CCBF8 and it_802CCC68 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed void functions, and exposed f32 data symbols it_804DD3AC and it_804DD3A8 as shown in the diff.
smart_moves: Introduced a Thunder-specific item-var struct and union member before using item->xDD4_itemVar.thunder fields, which makes the decompiled behavior more readable and avoids repeated raw-offset access.
Separated Thunder Pokemon special attributes from older/incorrect itThunderAttributes naming, clarifying that the item reads behavior parameters from xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes.
Used small static helper functions for repeated transition/setup code in UnkMotion0_Phys and UnkMotion2_Anim; this likely helped reproduce compiler output while keeping the matched C understandable.
Preserved null-safe JObj child traversal patterns explicitly instead of collapsing them into shorter C, which is often necessary for matching generated control flow.
Modeled effect lifetime through item callbacks: hitlag pause/resume callbacks use efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll, while delayed accessory effect spawning is handled through item->on_accessory.
Connected the Thunder projectile/visual motion to stage blast-zone logic by returning true from the anim callback once pos.y exceeds Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset().
decomp_lessons: For item decomp, adding the correct xDD4 item-var union member can unlock cleaner, matchable code and reduce reliance on anonymous padding or raw offsets.
Pokemon item special attributes may need item-specific structs even when field names are still unknown; x0/x4/x8/xC placeholders are acceptable when evidence only supports offsets and types.
Do not over-simplify JObj traversal when matching; the explicit null checks around jobj->child and child->child can be part of the required code shape.
State-transition helper blocks that set Item_80268E5C, hitlag callbacks, itcmd vars, and item vars are reusable patterns across item motion states.
Accessory callbacks can be used for delayed one-shot effects: gate with an item var flag, decrement a timer, clear the flag, then spawn an effect on a specific dynamic bone.
Blast-zone checks can appear in item animation callbacks rather than collision callbacks; future item work should look for Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset-style termination conditions in anim functions.
naming_conventions: Thunder item variable struct was named itThunder_ItemVars, matching the project pattern for item-specific xDD4 vars such as itFreezer_ItemVars, itFushigibana_ItemVars, and itkireihana_ItemVars.
Thunder Pokemon special attributes were named itThunderPokemonAttributes, using item/Pokemon-specific context while retaining unknown field names x0, x4, x8, and xC.
Unknown Thunder motion callbacks retained the existing itThunder_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll naming pattern.
Address-based unknown helper symbols remained as it_802CCBF8 and it_802CCC68 once signatures were recovered but semantic names were not yet established.
Temporary static matching helpers used the parent function name plus _inline or _inline2, indicating compiler-driven helper extraction rather than confirmed game semantics.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added the exact ItemStateTable data definition for it_803F7C40 to match .data, with state IDs 0, 1, and 2 and their anim/phys/coll callback triples.
Recovered typed signatures for formerly unknown functions in itthunder.h: it_802CCBF8(Item_GObj*) and it_802CCC68(HSD_GObj*).
Used explicit conditional null assignment around HSD_JObj child traversal to preserve likely compiler branch structure.
Introduced static helper functions around repeated setup sequences, likely to match inlining or compiler code-generation boundaries reported as inline helpers in the diff.
Used item->xDD4_itemVar.thunder.x60/x64/x68 to map offsets xE34/xE38/xE3C, aligning C field accesses with known item memory layout.
Used efSync_Spawn with effect ID 0x460, dynamic bone table bone index 2, and a local f32 out = 1.0f for the accessory effect path.
Set hitlag callbacks directly to efLib_PauseAll and efLib_ResumeAll in state setup functions, matching effect pause/resume behavior during item hitlag.

The match report indicates full matches for itThunder_UnkMotion2_Anim (+216 bytes), itThunder_UnkMotion0_Phys (+180 bytes), itThunder_UnkMotion1_Anim (+120 bytes), it_802CCC68 (+92 bytes), and .data (+48 bytes), with partial matches for it_802CCBF8 and itThunder_UnkMotion2_Phys.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The only human text excerpt was an automated GALE01 match report showing matched code/data improvements; it did not include reviewer guidance.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs touching src/melee/it/items/itthunder.c for final matches of it_802CCBF8 and itThunder_UnkMotion2_Phys, since this PR only improved them to 95.54% and 59.06% respectively.
Search for subsequent cleanup of it_804DD3AC and it_804DD3A8 definitions, because the diff excerpt shows f32 data definitions added in itthunder.h rather than only extern declarations.
Compare other Pokemon item decomp PRs for similar xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes structs and xDD4 item-var union additions.
Look for review or follow-up discussion on whether itThunderPokemonAttributes field names x0/x4/x8/xC were later given semantic names.
Search item accessory callback patterns using on_accessory plus efSync_Spawn to reuse the delayed effect countdown approach.
